user.description: Enter the local IP or mDNS name of your Wallbox Gateway. Username + password are only required if you enabled web auth on the gateway's /config page.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Wallbox Gateway
reconfigure.description: Update the gateway address, credentials, or poll interval. Leave the password blank to keep the current one.

reauth_confirm.title: Wallbox Gateway authentication
reauth_confirm.description: The gateway at {host} is rejecting the stored credentials. Enter the current web username and password.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the gateway at that address.
error.wrong_device: That gateway reports a different charger serial than this entry. Point it at the original charger, or add it as a new entry.

init.title: Charge Assistant
init.description: Pick what the assistant should do. Reminder nudges you to plug the car in.

triggers.title: When should I remind you?
triggers.description: Tick any combination. The next step only asks for what these need.

setup.title: Trigger settings
setup.description: Fill in the details for the triggers you chose.

data.arrival_entity: Presence entity (person or phone) that means you're home
data.tariff_below: Remind when price at or below

conditions.title: Conditions
conditions.description: Optional guards. Leave blank/off to skip.

data.skip_above_pct: Skip when battery at or above (%)
data.soc_max_age_min: Ignore battery level if older than (min)
data.only_if_scheduled: Only remind if a charge is actually scheduled
data.quiet_start: Quiet hours start (set = end to disable)

notify.title: Notification
notify.description: Where to send the reminder and how it behaves.

data.title: Notification title
data.escalate_min: Re-remind after (min) if still unplugged (0 = off)

target.title: Smart charge — charge to a target
target.description: Stops charging once the car reaches your target. Optionally also starts it. Auto-start is off by default so it won't charge (cost money) until you choose. Set a departure time to charge just-in-time so it's ready by then (needs battery size + charge power).

data.target_autostart: Also start charging when below target (and plugged in)
data.battery_kwh: Battery capacity (kWh) — for the ready-by estimate
data.charge_power_kw: Typical charge power (kW) — for the ready-by estimate
data.cheapest_window: Charge in the cheapest hours (needs a price-forecast sensor)
data.price_entity: Electricity price sensor with a forecast (Amber / Nord Pool / Tibber)
data.trip_target_pct: Trip target charge level (%) — for an upcoming trip
data.trip_until: Trip target applies until (then reverts to the everyday target)
data.price_cap: Never charge above this price (your departure time still wins)
data.window_enabled: Only charge during a set window (cheap hours)
data.window_end: Window end (local time, can cross midnight)
data.window_overrun: Keep charging past the window until the target is reached
data.window_prestart: Start before the window if needed to be ready by departure
data.window_cost_warn: Notify me when a charge runs outside the window

smart_solar.title: Smart + Solar — solar first, grid to finish
smart_solar.description: Charges from excess solar whenever it's available (free), and tops up from grid only inside your cheap window — or just in time to reach the target by departure. Stops at the target.

data.battery_kwh: Battery capacity (kWh) — for the ready-by / pre-start estimate
data.surplus_source: Surplus source (a sensor, or derive from grid / solar−load)
data.grid_entity: Grid power sensor (for 'Compute from grid power')
data.window_enabled: Only top up from grid during a set window (cheap hours)
data.window_end: Window end (local time, can cross midnight)
data.window_overrun: Keep charging past the window until the target is reached
data.window_prestart: Start before the window if needed to be ready by departure
data.window_cost_warn: Notify me when a charge runs outside the window

solar.title: Solar charge — charge from excess solar
solar.description: Starts charging when your solar surplus stays above the start level, and stops when it drops below the stop level. Thresholds are in your surplus sensor's own units (kW or W). For finer current-following, the charger's native Eco-Smart 'Full green' mode is an alternative.

data.surplus_debounce_min: Must hold this long before acting (min)
data.surplus_source: Surplus source (a sensor, or derive from grid / solar−load)
data.grid_entity: Grid power sensor (for 'Compute from grid power')
data.solar_dynamic: Follow surplus by adjusting charge current (advanced/experimental)
data.load_power_entity: House/grid power sensor (for the load limit)
data.window_enabled: Only charge during a set window (cheap hours)
data.window_end: Window end (local time, can cross midnight)
data.window_overrun: Keep charging past the window until the target is reached
data.window_prestart: Start before the window if needed to be ready by departure
data.window_cost_warn: Notify me when a charge runs outside the window

not_control_owner.title: Charge Assistant isn't the charge-control owner
not_control_owner.description:
You've set up a smart-charge / solar mode, but the gateway's **Charge Control Owner** is set to `{owner}`, so the Charge Assistant is standing down and won't start or stop charging.

To let it take over, open the gateway's config page (http://<gateway-ip>/config) and set **Charge Control Owner** to **Home Assistant Integration**.
